events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: The legendary Beatles drummer, Ringo Starr, celebrates his 86th birthday today.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Happy 86th birthday to the one and only Ringo Starr! From Abbey Road to his solo hits, his beat is unmistakable. What's your favourite Ringo moment?

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's iconic guitarist and astrophysicist, Brian May, celebrates his 79th birthday.

angles.name: Guitar God
angles.copy: Happy 79th to the legendary Brian May! His guitar riffs with Queen are pure magic. What's the Queen track that instantly gets you air-guitaring?

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The charismatic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Mick Jagger, celebrates his 83rd birthday.

angles.name: Ageless Rocker
angles.copy: Still got the moves at 83! Happy birthday to the incredible Mick Jagger. What's your all-time favourite Rolling Stones anthem?

events.title: MTV launched in 1981
events.blurb: On this day in 1981, MTV launched with its first video, The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star', forever changing the music landscape.

angles.name: MTV's Debut
angles.copy: 45 years ago today, MTV launched and changed music forever! Kicking off with 'Video Killed The Radio Star' – quite the ironic start for a channel that made the video star. What was the first music video that blew your mind?

events.title: Oasis' 'Definitely Maybe' released (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their iconic debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, launching them into Britpop superstardom.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Day
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe' and changed everything. From 'Supersonic' to 'Live Forever', which track still gives you chills?

events.title: Woodstock Festival began (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, commenced on August 15, 1969, bringing '3 Days of Peace & Music'.

angles.name: Remembering Woodstock
angles.copy: 57 years ago, Woodstock began! Three days of peace, love, and music that defined a generation. If you could have been there, who would you have wanted to see?

events.title: Anniversary of Elvis Presley's death
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the passing of the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who died on August 16, 1977.

angles.name: King's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering Elvis Presley, who left us on this day in 1977. His music and iconic style continue to influence generations. What's your favourite Elvis track?

events.title: Anniversary of Tupac Shakur's shooting
events.blurb: Today marks the anniversary of the shooting incident involving hip-hop icon Tupac Shakur in Las Vegas in 1996, which tragically led to his death on September 13.

angles.name: Tupac's Impact
angles.copy: Remembering Tupac Shakur on the anniversary of the tragic shooting in 1996. His powerful lyrics and legacy continue to resonate. What's his most impactful song for you?

events.title: The Beatles' 'Abbey Road' released (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles' iconic album 'Abbey Road' was released in the UK on September 26, 1969, featuring classic tracks and that famous zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Abbey Road at 57
angles.copy: It's 57 years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. From 'Come Together' to the epic medley, it's a masterpiece. Which track is your go-to?

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the formidable frontwoman of The Pretenders, celebrates her 75th birthday.

angles.name: Pretenders Power
angles.copy: Happy 75th birthday to rock icon Chrissie Hynde! Her voice and attitude with The Pretenders are truly legendary. What's your favourite Pretenders hit?

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The distinctive voice of Oasis and a Britpop icon, Liam Gallagher, celebrates his 54th birthday.

angles.name: Mad for Liam
angles.copy: Happy 54th birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher! The swagger, the voice, the anthems. What's your favourite Liam moment, solo or with Oasis?

events.title: John Lennon's 'Imagine' released (1975 UK single)
events.blurb: John Lennon's timeless anthem 'Imagine' was released as a single in the UK on October 24, 1975, though the album and US single came out in 1971.

angles.name: Imagine at 51
angles.copy: 51 years since John Lennon released 'Imagine' as a single in the UK. Its message of peace and unity is as powerful today as ever. What does this song mean to you?

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: The first Apple iPod, which revolutionised portable music, was unveiled on October 23, 2001, and released on November 10, 2001.

angles.name: iPod Revolution
angles.copy: Remember the first iPod? It changed how we listened to music forever! What was the first album you loaded onto your shiny new iPod?

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated as James Bond Day, marking the anniversary of the world premiere of the first Bond film, Dr. No, in 1962.

angles.name: Bond, James Bond
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! From iconic theme tunes to thrilling gadgets, 007 always delivers. Who's your favourite Bond, and what's the best Bond song?

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: Today marks what would have been the 86th birthday of The Beatles' co-founder, John Lennon.

angles.name: Remembering John Lennon
angles.copy: Remembering the visionary John Lennon, who would have been 86 today. His music and message of peace continue to inspire. What's your favourite Lennon song?
